text:
<think>I should begin with a general statement about why the sky has different colors at different times of the day.</think>
The color of the sky changes due to the way sunlight interacts with Earth's atmosphere. 
<think>Now I should explain the basic concept of scattering and why blue is seen during the daytime.</think>
During the day, sunlight passes through less atmosphere, and shorter blue wavelengths are scattered in all directions by the gases and particles in the air. 
<think>Next, I should clarify why red and orange colors dominate at sunset.</think>
At sunset, the sun is lower in the sky, so its light must travel through a thicker layer of the atmosphere, which scatters away the shorter blue wavelengths and allows the longer red and orange wavelengths to reach our eyes. 
<think>Finally, I should summarize the key idea in a concluding sentence.</think>
This process, known as Rayleigh scattering, explains why the sky is blue during most of the day but appears red or orange when the sun is near the horizon.
